product_updates.title: Pentagon moves to designate Anthropic as a supply-chain risk
product_updates.content: "We don't need it, we don't want it, and will not do business with them again," the president wrote in the post.

product_updates.title: Musk bashes OpenAI in deposition, saying ‘nobody committed suicide because of Grok’
product_updates.content: In his lawsuit against OpenAI, Musk touted xAI safety compared with ChatGPT. A few months later, xAI's Grok flooded X with nonconsensual nude images.

product_updates.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon: What’s actually at stake?
product_updates.content: Anthropic and the Pentagon are clashing over AI use in autonomous weapons and surveillance, raising high-stakes questions about national security, corporate control, and who sets the rules for military AI.

product_updates.title: ChatGPT reaches 900M weekly active users
product_updates.content: OpenAI shared the new numbers as part of its announcement that it has raised $110 billion in private funding.

product_updates.title: Who’s really running AI? Inside the billion-dollar battle over regulation with Alex Bores
product_updates.content: The Pentagon is playing chicken with Anthropic over who gets to control how the military uses AI while communities across the country are blocking data center construction. As the AI debate has been flattened to “doomers versus boomers,” one state legislator is attempting to walk a middle road. O…

product_updates.title: AI music generator Suno hits 2M paid subscribers and $300M in annual recurring revenue
product_updates.content: Suno lets users create music using natural language prompts, making it possible for people with little experience to generate audio with little effort.

product_updates.title: Perplexity’s new Computer is another bet that users need many AI models
product_updates.content: Perplexity Computer, in the company’s words, "unifies every current AI capability into a single system."

product_updates.title: Employees at Google and OpenAI support Anthropic’s Pentagon stand in open letter
product_updates.content: While Anthropic has an existing partnership with the Pentagon, the AI company has remained firm that its technology not be used for mass domestic surveillance or fully autonomous weaponry.

product_updates.title: Last 24 hours to get TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets at the lowest rates of the year
product_updates.content: The lowest rates of the year for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 end after today. Prices go up at 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't miss connecting with 10,000 founders, investors, and operators, and key takeaways from 250+ industry leaders. Register now to save up to $680, or up to 30% on group passes.

product_updates.title: OpenAI raises $110B in one of the largest private funding rounds in history
product_updates.content: The new funding consists of a $50 billion investment from Amazon as well as $30 billion each from Nvidia and SoftBank, against a $730 billion valuation.

technical_breakthroughs.title: MIT Technology Review is a 2026 ASME finalist in reporting
technical_breakthroughs.content: The American Society of Magazine Editors has named MIT Technology Review as a finalist for a 2026 National Magazine Award in the reporting category. The shortlisted story—“We did the math on AI’s energy footprint. Here’s the story you haven’t heard”—is part of the publication’s Power Hungry packa…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: how AI is shaking up Go, and a cybersecurity mystery
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: AI is rewiring how the world’s best Go players think
technical_breakthroughs.content: Burrowed in the alleys of Hongik-dong, a hushed residential neighborhood in eastern Seoul, is a faded stone-tiled building stamped “Korea Baduk Association,” the governing body for professional Go. The game is an ancient one, with sacred stature in South Korea. But inside the building, rooms once…
